segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a bright white screen before transitioning to the vibrant America's Got Talent Season 16 stage, featuring a large illuminated star backdrop and a panel of judges seated at a table. Dynamic, electronic music with a strong beat begins as Léa Kyle enters in a sleek black outfit, striking a confident pose. She starts her act by interacting with a large modular screen structure displaying abstract visuals, then swiftly changes into a shimmering blue dress. As she performs a fluid dance routine, manipulating colorful blue, red, and white fabric pieces, the judges and audience react with exclamations like "Whoa!" and "Oh!", with one voice mimicking "Swish, swish, swish." Léa transitions into a silver and black ensemble, continuing her expressive performance, prompting more surprised "Ah!" and "Oh!" reactions from the judges, including Sofia clapping and Howie smiling broadly. The background music features lyrics, "Only my name keeps coming at your mind, 'cause I stay with you," as the judges, visibly engaged, exclaim "Wow!" and "Yeah!". Léa Kyle concludes her act in a black strapless dress, holding red pom-poms, striking a final pose to a final "Woo!" from the audience and judges.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens on a brightly lit stage with a vibrant, geometric light backdrop featuring the 'Britain's Got Talent' logo, as the male performer from Matricks Illusions begins to narrate, explaining that "For years and years, many illusionists, magicians, all over the world" have performed "the beautiful art form called quick change." He describes how traditional quick change would use "a hoop like this, a beautiful costume and a girl, and the costume would change like this." As he speaks, the female performer, initially in a black sleeveless dress, stands on a table while two assistants prepare a red cloth. The male performer then declares, "Pretty cool, right? But this is Britain's Got Talent, and we like to do things differently. This is the human quick change!" The assistants lift the red cloth, dramatically revealing the female performer in a striking red sequined outfit, which she showcases with raised arms to the building, upbeat electronic music and growing audience applause. The scene transitions to a large, transparent box where the female performer, now in a red corset and white shirt, appears to be transformed into a man wearing sunglasses and a white shirt, creating a visual illusion that elicits a loud "Whoa!" from the audience and judges. Close-ups show Simon, Amanda, and David visibly amused, laughing and applauding. Simon comments, "I had to have a quick look to make sure he was still there," followed by Amanda's excited "Woo!" The performer then exits the box, and the illusion concludes with a final reveal of the male assistant inside the box, dressed in the same red corset and white shirt, as the celebratory electronic music continues and the audience cheers. The dynamic stage lighting with colorful beams enhances the theatrical atmosphere throughout.

Jeki, dressed in a beige trench coat and black hat, dramatically pushes open a large wooden door marked 'DO NOT ENTER', revealing Ellie in a black-and-white polka dot dress with a fur-trimmed hood. A male voice exclaims, "How did they do that? What?" as a "whoosh" and magical sound effect accompany the reveal. The stage is illuminated with vibrant blue and purple lights, reflecting off the glossy floor, against a stylized London skyline backdrop. The music shifts to a mysterious, rhythmic orchestral piece as Jeki manipulates a large black fabric with a long pole, creating dramatic visual effects. Ellie joins him for synchronized movements, captivating the audience. The judges—Simon, Amanda, Alesha, and David—are seen reacting with visible amusement and surprise, their 'X' buttons all lit. David exclaims, "He's got a gadget!" and Amanda adds a "Woo-hoo!" as the act progresses. The music builds to a dramatic crescendo, prompting Simon to exclaim, "Oh, quick, quick!" and David to follow with, "Oh, wait, wait!" The segment concludes with a wide shot of the stage, showcasing Ellie & Jeki in the center, the judges' table in the foreground, and the prominent BRITAIN'S GOT TALENT Logo, all set to a grand, triumphant orchestral piece and continued crowd cheering. On-screen text identifies the segment as a 'TALENT RECAP' featuring 'Ellie & Jeki' from 'Britain's Got Talent Series 12'.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a woman with curly hair in a floral top, holding a phone and reacting with surprise and amusement, set against twinkling lights and a brick wall. A distinct female voice is heard humorously asking, "Is Beyoncé always on this? It's Tyra Banks," and later instructing, "Blue Ivy, Blue Ivy, sit down right now. Mama got to host this show, Blue," accompanied by audience laughter and applause. The scene transitions to the judges’ table where Simon, in a black shirt, and Howie, in a dark suit, are seated. Simon reads a tweet aloud from his phone, his voice clearly stating, "AGT made Simon Cowell so soft," which prompts Howie to interject with "Easy reader" and Simon to respond, "Why am I..." Howie then adds, "Yes, for me." Simon continues reading the tweet, "I want Simon the savage back. The one who crushed dreams and made people cry for fun," eliciting more laughter from the audience and Howie, who exclaims, "Ha ha ha!" The setting shifts to a different judging panel where Simon, now in a white shirt, is seen laughing and interacting with Howie, in a white jacket with black stripes, and Sofia, in a pink dress, as they react to a performance. Howie dramatically announces, "Password is... magic," leading to a playful argument with Sofia, who asks, "Did you just look?" Simon denies, "No," and Howie adds, "Not at the..." Sofia presses, "Are you guys talking?" to which Simon again replies, "No." Howie then whispers "Trick" and "Magic" as the word 'MAGIC' appears on screen. The judges, including Heidi in a blue sequined dress and Amanda in a red top, express excitement and surprise with shouts of "Yes!" and enthusiastic audience cheers. Howie excitedly declares, "Magic is the correct word!" The judges’ table is adorned with branded mugs from Dunkin’ Donuts and Sonic. A lively, playful argument ensues, with Howie accusing, "That is cheating! Who heard him tell me the word?" Sofia echoes, "That's cheating!" Simon retorts, "No, you were cheating!" and Sofia fires back, "No, you were cheating!" Heidi chimes in with "Already!" and Sofia adds, "Send him!" Howie insists, "They're cheating, Heidi, he..." but Heidi counters, "Because they heard you cheating!" Simon and Howie stand up, gesturing animatedly, while Sofia and Heidi continue to react with wide-eyed enthusiasm, amidst continued audience applause and the judges' playful banter, creating a lively and theatrical atmosphere with dynamic lighting.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a host in a teal suit, holding a black folder, addressing a live audience in a dimly lit theater with blue stage lighting. He announces, "This is the final clue. We are actually tied at the moment and we are going to start with Howie." The scene cuts to the judges' table, where Sofia, in a pink dress, and another female judge in a silver top are seated, each with a red Sonic-branded cup. The host prompts, "The password is... Ready?" Howie, in a white jacket with black stripes, confidently states, "Million." The host confirms, "Dollars," as the audience reacts with laughter and applause. Howie repeats "Million," prompting a burst of cheers from the audience. The host exclaims, "Oh! He's right!" Simon, in a white shirt, points emphatically while Howie raises his hand in surprise. The host declares, "Thank you very much everybody. That is the word! Simon and Howie have won!" The audience erupts in cheers, and a female voice, likely Sofia, excitedly adds, "I wanted to say that!" The host thanks them for playing 'password' and reiterates, "Million dollars!" A blue hexagonal graphic with the word 'MILLION' appears on screen amidst continued audience cheers. Simon then playfully asks, "Where's the check? Where's the check?" The scene transitions to a new setting with warm red curtains and hanging Edison bulbs, creating an intimate, interview-style atmosphere. Here, Simon, now in a dark blue shirt, and Howie, in a black suit, are seated in director’s chairs on a reflective stage floor, engaged in conversation with the host, who is now in a light blue suit. The host gestures energetically between them, explaining a 'mind-reading technique' to show they are 'in perfect sync.' Simon listens thoughtfully with a hand-to-chin gesture, while Howie listens with arms crossed, interjecting "Clear your minds" and "Okay" during the host's explanation.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a vibrant, theatrical setting with rich red curtains, gold trim, and hanging Edison bulbs, creating a warm, intimate ambiance. A woman with long blonde hair, wearing a grey strapless dress and large silver earrings, adjusts her hair and smiles confidently. The scene cuts to a wide shot revealing a host in a light blue suit standing between two seated women—one in a shimmering orange dress and the other in the grey dress—on director’s chairs. The host counts down, "Three, two, one!" before calling out "Shoes!" and "Earring and shoes!", prompting the women to react with excitement, raising their arms and laughing as on-screen text labels 'SHOES' and 'EARRINGS' appear. The host continues, directing them to "Look at each other deeply in the eyes" and "understand each other," leading further rounds with countdowns and calls of "Necklace!" and "Lipstick!". This elicits loud screams and laughter from the women, with the host exclaiming, "This is freaking lipstick!". After the host states, "You have the power," and some brief dialogue, the scene shifts to include two men seated in director’s chairs: a bald man with glasses in a black suit, and a man with short dark hair in a black t-shirt. The host counts down again, then calls out "Handsome!", as on-screen text 'HANDSOME' appears. When 'JERK' appears, the host questions, "What? Did you say handsome? Handsome!", prompting the bald man to exclaim, "This is crazy!" and the man in the t-shirt to deny, "That was not, by the way," and "I swear on my life that was not planned." The segment transitions to a new setting with a neon 'America's Got Talent' backdrop, where the bald man, now wearing orange-framed glasses, is asked by a female voice, "what profession for three years?". With on-screen text displaying the question and options, he enthusiastically answers, "I know what it was. Dentistry," explaining, "because she has amazing teeth," to which the female voice confirms, "She's going to be a dentist." The warm, theatrical lighting and glossy reflective floor enhance the stage-like atmosphere throughout.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with close-ups of America's Got Talent judges Simon, appearing thoughtful in a dark blue shirt, and Sofia, smiling warmly in a black top, seated in director’s chairs against a vibrant backdrop featuring the show’s neon logo and red curtains with Edison bulbs. Howie's voice is heard explaining, "I want to know why I know that? They don't show this backstage. She will actually floss my teeth," as the scene briefly transitions to a dental office with a blue dental chair and equipment before cutting back to the judges. Simon's voice then adds, "Imagine if you turn up and guess who's your dentist, Sofia Vergara," to which Sofia's voice playfully responds, "That sounds so sexy." Simon's voice continues, "It'd be like, you know what, I'll have a filling out a week." The scene cuts to Sofia, who, with an on-screen question asking 'How many Emmys have I been nominated for?' and options A. Two, B. Four, C. Seven, asks in her own voice, "How many Emmys have I been nominated for?" Howie reacts with a surprised expression. Simon's voice guesses, "I'm gonna say seven," followed by Howie's voice suggesting, "Two Emmys." Sofia's voice then states, "Seven times, let's just go for the seven." A distinct buzzer sound is heard, followed by Howie's voice asking, "Why are you looking at me like that?" and Simon's voice responding, "I'm guessing, I don't know." Sofia's voice then reveals, "Four." Howie's voice interjects, "You didn't let me finish. I was going to say two, times two." Simon's voice concludes, "Well, she should have got more." The scene then shifts to a live performance setting, accompanied by upbeat, energetic music and enthusiastic applause, showing the judges seated at a long white table with Sonic-branded mugs. The host, in a grey suit, stands and speaks animatedly to the audience, stating, "The competition was brutal," while Howie gestures expressively. The host continues, "The and and last night, we had the highest of highs and then some incredible for me, personal disappointments." Addressing the judges and audience, visible under dim, blue-toned stage lighting, the host declares, "And we are here and we are all paid, to be honest. I am brutally honest. That's why it was brutal. Good luck to everyone." Sofia's voice interjects, "But you sometimes are also grumpy," to which the host immediately retorts, "I'm not grumpy." This exchange is met with laughter and audience reaction. The video concludes with a still image of Sofia in a white strapless gown on the Emmys red carpet, posing with her back to the camera, showcasing her long blonde hair and a bracelet, with the NBC and Emmys logos in the background, as the host's voice is heard saying, "I'm also..." before the segment ends.

Sofia responds, explaining that 'first of all, we have different taste,' to which Howie interjects, claiming, 'We all have the same taste.' Sofia continues, suggesting some judges 'don't know what they're supposed to know,' prompting Simon, a man in a dark t-shirt, to state, 'I know everything we know.' The scene shifts to a different set of judges, including Mel B, a woman with a pink turban in a white ruffled top, who laughs and gestures. A female voice, identified as Mel B, then exclaims, 'Welcome to Buzzer Buddy!' accompanied by a distinct buzzer sound. She then playfully questions Howie about what he'd throw away first from 'Simon's closet.' Howie, seen raising his hand and speaking, responds by narrowing it down to 'the white t-shirt and the white dress shirt,' while Mel B adds 'or the bell-bottom pants,' both agreeing that Simon 'wears the same thing.' An on-screen graphic appears, displaying a score of 'HEIDI MEL B 2' and 'HOWIE SIMON 1' with cartoon caricatures, reinforcing the interactive and competitive atmosphere as the audience continues to clap.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a wide shot of the America's Got Talent judges' table, featuring Howie, Simon, and Heidi. Simon, in a white shirt, speaks animatedly into his microphone with a distinct British accent, praising Howie as "one of the most amazing people I've ever worked with," and expressing his desire "to pay you a tribute to show your significance and most importantly, your importance for the 10 years you've been on the show." Howie, in a black pinstripe suit and orange-framed glasses, listens intently. The camera cuts to a close-up of Simon, emphasizing his expressive hand movements as he concludes, "Over to you, Terry," prompting immediate audience applause and cheers. The scene then shifts to the host, Terry Crews, a bald man in a shiny black suit, standing center stage against a vibrant cityscape backdrop. Terry, speaking with an energetic American accent, gestures dramatically toward the audience, building anticipation by announcing, "It's out on Sunset Boulevard, okay? Let's take a look." As he encourages, "Count it down with me, people!", Howie's voice is heard asking, "Where am I looking?" The camera pulls back to reveal a large stage with a countdown timer, and the host, joined by the audience, loudly counts down from "Five! Four! Three! Two! One!" A dramatic sound effect accompanies the reveal of a massive screen displaying a billboard that reads 'Happy 10 YEAR ANNIVERSARY Howie!' with his photo. The audience erupts in massive applause and cheers, with Howie visibly moved, laughing and clapping, exclaiming, "What? Is that real?" Simon also laughs heartily, raising his hand in celebration, as Terry confirms, "Yes!" The segment culminates with Simon and Howie sharing a warm, heartfelt hug, both beaming with joy, as the audience continues to cheer, marking a significant and emotional milestone for Howie’s tenure on the show.
